title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health to support the Joslin Diabetes Center.
Diagnosed with Type 1 Diabetes at age 11, Bennett credits Joslin Diabetes Center with helping maintain his sports activities.
Bennett aims to inspire others with diabetes, running to symbolize overcoming the challenges faced by young diabetes patients.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter receives her fifth Oscar nomination for costume design in 'Sinners,' setting a record for Black women.
Carter was the first Black person to win an Oscar for Best Costume Design in 2019 and the first Black woman to win multiple Oscars by 2023.
She discusses her process, working remotely from Massachusetts, and designing costumes for Denzel Washington's upcoming project with Netflix.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools face potential 210 job cuts if a property-tax override fails, say officials.
The district needs $18.5 million over three years to maintain current staffing and services.
Brookline voters will decide on the proposed override during the May 5 annual town election.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The PETS Act aims to ban the sale of dogs and cats in Massachusetts pet shops.
The legislation encourages partnerships between pet shops and shelters to facilitate animal adoptions.
The bill seeks to eliminate housing and insurance barriers to pet ownership in Massachusetts.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts faces persistent drought despite significant February snowfall, prompting state officials to urge water conservation.
Central and Northeast Massachusetts are at Level 3 Critical Drought, while Western Massachusetts is at Level 1 Mild Drought.
Residents are advised to conserve water through household measures like shorter showers and checking for leaks.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Women obscuring boyfriends online signifies a shift in prioritizing male partners in public identities.
Chanté Joseph's viral essay explores modern relationships and challenges traditional heterosexual norms.
Joseph discusses societal pressures on women to partner, questioning the default of coupledom.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O'Neill is running the Boston Marathon to support The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp.
The camp provides a medically safe environment for children with serious conditions to enjoy camp activities.
O'Neill's fiancée's sister, a former camper, now serves as a counselor after recovering from bone cancer.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Philadelphia Eagles will not trade A.J. Brown to the New England Patriots at this time, according to reports.
Trade discussions may resume closer to June 1 for salary cap relief, impacting Brown's dead cap hit and space for Eagles.
The Patriots previously offered a first- and third-round pick for Brown, which the Eagles have reportedly denied.

title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum is set to return for the Celtics against the Wizards after missing a game for injury management.
Tatum, recovering from a right ruptured Achilles, is playing with a minutes restriction determined by the Celtics' sport science team.
Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable, while Nikola Vucevic remains out for the Celtics' game against the Wizards.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'War Machine', featuring Alan Ritchson as a PTSD-affected soldier, is now streaming on Netflix.
'Zootopia 2', starring Jason Bateman and Ginnifer Goodwin, is available on Disney+.
'One Piece' Season 2 on Netflix follows Luffy's adventures in the Grand Line.
